_meta.description: Canonical alias map for Koda data banks. Provides backward-compatible bank ID resolution and safe migration support when banks are renamed, moved, split, or merged. Aliases must never change meaning; they only redirect to the canonical bank id.

config._comment: Strict aliasing prevents ambiguous routing. One alias → one canonical id.
config._comment2: If an alias points to a bank that doesn't exist, boot must fail in prod/staging.
config._comment3: If canonical id exists but alias is duplicate or colliding, boot must fail.
config._comment4: Optional: allow old bank IDs to remain valid for a grace period (migration windows).

aliases.reason: Canonical id (self-alias for stability).
Some earlier builds stored this bank under quality/ or formatting/. Canonical stays 'banned_phrases' regardless of folder.
Legacy name used before 'clarification_phrases' became standard.
Earlier builds used 'scoped' suffix; canonical policy uses 'scope'.
Old microcopy filename. Now points to processing_messages.
Earlier bank name used in older runtimePatterns loaders.
Singular form used by older code; canonical uses plural.

normalizationRules._comment: Alias key normalization rules. Applied to lookup only — canonical ids are unchanged.

tests._comment: These are deterministic and should be enforced at boot.

cases.assert: all aliases[].alias are unique after normalization
no alias points to another alias (canonicalId must be a canonical bank id)
expiresInDays is null or within config.migrationPolicy.maxGraceDays
